name: GitHub Primer
description: GitHub's design-system primitives: base color scales plus a functional token layer (bgColor/fgColor/borderColor) that powers github.com across nine themes. One of the most battle-tested dark modes in existence, running at massive scale.
philosophy: Base scales are hand-tuned per theme rather than algorithmically generated or inverted: light and dark each get their own re-tuned 0-9 chromatic ramps, and the neutral additionally extends to 13 in the source. The dark neutral runs in the opposite direction (black-first) so light and dark themes can share most functional tokens without overrides. Steps are contrast-anchored: neutral 0-5 are backgrounds (rest/hover/active for controls), 7-8 borders (8 is the minimum contrast for interactive control borders against bgColor-muted), 9-10 text/icons (9 is minimum text contrast against backgrounds 0-4). Raw steps are never used directly in product code; everything goes through functional tokens, and every chromatic scale places its 'emphasis' color at step 5 in both themes.
notes: Primer is intentionally non-uniform in its source: chromatic scales have 10 steps (0-9) but the neutral extends to 14 (0-13). To keep the uniform format, the neutral is truncated here to its verbatim steps 0-9; the omitted dark end (light: 10 #454C54, 11 #393F46, 12 #25292E, 13 #1f2328 / dark: 10 #B7BDC8, 11 #D1D7E0, 12 #F0F6FC, 13 #ffffff) carries Primer's real default text color — fgColor-default is neutral 13 (#1f2328) in light and 12 (#f0f6fc) in dark — so roleMapping.text falls back to 9, the docs' minimum-contrast text step and exactly fgColor-muted (#59636e light / #9198a1 dark); text and textSubtle therefore share an index. Other roles are grounded in the light theme's functional tokens (bgColor-default=neutral 0, bgColor-muted/control-rest=1, hover=2, active=3, borderColor-default=6-7, borderColor-emphasis=8) plus the docs' step guidance; the dark theme shifts some by a step (appBg=1 since dark neutral 0 #010409 is bgColor-inset, borders 7-8). solid=5 / solidHover=6 hold for chromatic scales in both themes (bgColor-accent/danger/attention-emphasis = step 5); the neutral solid (bgColor-neutral-emphasis) uses step 9 light / 8 dark. Exceptions off the base scales: bgColor-success-emphasis (primary button green) is a custom #1f883d (light) / #238636-adjacent ramp value, and muted borders/backgrounds use alpha variants (e.g. borderColor-muted #d1d9e0b3, borderColor-accent-muted #54aeff66) that are not separate scale steps. fgColor-onEmphasis is #ffffff in both themes. The functional CSS also ships separate 10-step per-theme 'display' scales for data visualization, and there are 9 themes total (light/dark plus dimmed, high-contrast, colorblind, tritanopia variants), each re-tuning the same scale structure.
